## Settlers Of Crutan Frontend — AI Coding Agent Guide

Use these project-specific notes to work productively in this repo.

### Architecture

- React + TypeScript app built with Vite; routing via `react-router` data APIs.
- State: `zustand` store for auth in `src/auth/store.ts` (persisted to `sessionStorage`).
- API: generated OpenAPI types (`src/api/types.ts`) + `openapi-fetch` client in `src/api/client.ts` with `credentials: "include"` for cookie auth.
- Styling: Tailwind CSS v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite` and `src/index.css`.
- Dev proxy/Prod reverse-proxy: Vite dev proxies `/api` to backend; Nginx serves built assets and proxies `/api` in production.

### Dev Workflows

- Start backend first (Aspire environment) so OpenAPI and API are reachable.
- Run `npm run dev` (or `npm start`) to launch Vite. Scripts auto-run `types:api` before starting.
- After API changes, regenerate types: `npm run types:api` (writes `src/api/types.ts` from `http://localhost:5107/openapi/v1.json`). Do not edit this file by hand.
- Build: `npm run build` (runs `tsc -b` then `vite build`), Preview: `npm run preview`, Lint: `npm run lint`.

### Environment & Proxying

- `vite.config.ts` reads `VITE_PORT` and proxies `/api` to `process.env.services__api__https__0` (or `services__api__http__0`). Aspire sets these.
- Production Nginx uses `default.conf.template` to: serve SPA (history fallback) and proxy `/api` to `${services__api__https__0}` on port `${VITE_PORT}`.

### API Calling Pattern (typed)

- Use the shared client: `import { api } from "../api/client"`.
- Methods return `{ data, error, response }`. Always check `error` and pass typed params:

```ts
const { data, error } = await api.GET("/api/lobby/{lobbyId}", {
  params: { path: { lobbyId } },
});
if (error) throw error;
```

### Auth Model & Guards

- Auth store (`src/auth/store.ts`):
  - Actions: `init`, `refresh`, `login`, `logout` (cookie-based; server session).
  - Derived helpers: `isAuthed()`, `hasRole(role)`, `hasAnyRole(roles)`.
- Bootstrap (`src/components/AppBootstrap.tsx`): calls `init()` on mount, `refresh()` on window focus/visibility change.
- Route guards (`src/components/guards.tsx`): wrap protected UI with `<RequireAuth>` or `<RequireRole roles={["Admin"]}>`.

### Routing Pattern

- Router defined in `src/main.tsx` using data routers. Example loader that fetches via the typed client:

```ts
{
  path: "lobby/:lobbyId",
  loader: async ({ params }) =>
    api.GET("/api/lobby/{lobbyId}", { params: { path: { lobbyId: params.lobbyId! } } })
      .then(r => ({ data: r.data, status: r.response.status })),
  Component: Lobby,
}
```

### Conventions

- Keep backend calls under `/api/*` so dev/prod proxies work seamlessly.
- Use the generated OpenAPI types for request/response shapes; prefer `loader` for route data when possible.
- Persist only `auth.user` and `auth.status` (see `partialize` in the store). Use store selectors for conditional UI.
- Don’t modify generated `src/api/types.ts`; regenerate with `types:api` instead.

### Notable Files

- `src/api/client.ts`: shared OpenAPI client (same-origin, cookies included).
- `src/auth/store.ts`: auth state/flows; session persistence.
- `src/components/guards.tsx`: `<RequireAuth>` and `<RequireRole>` wrappers.
- `src/layouts/AppLayout.tsx`: top-level layout/nav driven by auth selectors.
- `vite.config.ts`: dev port/proxy; build configuration.
- `default.conf.template` and `Dockerfile`: Nginx SPA hosting + `/api` proxy in production.

If anything above is unclear or missing (e.g., additional roles, non-`/api` endpoints, or local env setup without Aspire), let me know and I’ll refine this guide.
